sdxl-base-1.0.style_notes: Standard SDXL baseline. Favor concise, comma-separated descriptive tags appended after the main sentence (e.g. 'cinematic lighting, intricate details, 8k'). Common SDXL quality boosters like 'masterpiece, best quality' are effective when appropriate.

sdxl-turbo.style_notes: Fast single/few-step SDXL variant. Keep prompts shorter and punchier; avoid excessive tag stacking — turbo models respond better to concise, direct phrasing than long comma-separated tag lists.

dreamshaper-xl.style_notes: Creative/fantasy-oriented checkpoint. Lean into vivid, imaginative, painterly descriptors; fantasy lighting and atmospheric mood terms work especially well.

juggernaut-xl.style_notes: Photorealistic checkpoint. Favor photographic terminology: camera/lens references, realistic lighting descriptions, skin and material texture detail; avoid painterly or illustration language.
